2603.29496.title: Metriplector: From Field Theory to Neural Architecture
2603.29496.paper_summary: We present Metriplector, a neural architecture primitive in which the input configures an abstract physical system -- fields, sources, and operators -- and the dynamics of that system is the computation. Multiple fields evolve via coupled metriplectic dynamics, and the stress-energy tensor $T^{μν}$, derived from Noether's theorem, provides the readout. The metriplectic formulation admits a natural spectrum of instantiations: the dissipative branch alone yields a screened Poisson equation solved exactly via conjugate gradient; activating the full structure -- including the antisymmetric Poisson bracket -- gives field dynamics for image recognition and language modeling. We evaluate Metriplector across four domains, each using a task-specific architecture built from this shared primitive with progressively richer physics: F1=1.0 on maze pathfinding, generalizing from 15x15 training grids to unseen 39x39 grids; 97.2% exact Sudoku solve rate with zero structural injection; 81.03% on CIFAR-100 with 2.26M parameters; and 1.182 bits/byte on language modeling with 3.6x fewer training tokens than a GPT baseline.
2603.29496.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 Metriplector，一種神經架構原語，其中輸入配置了一個抽象的物理系統——場、源和算子——而該系統的動力學即為計算。多個場通過耦合的 metriplectic 動力學演化，並且由 Noether 定理推導出的應力-能量張量 $T^{μν}$ 提供讀出結果。metriplectic 公式允許自然的實例化光譜：僅耗散分支就產生了一個屏蔽的泊松方程，通過共軛梯度精確求解；啟用完整結構——包括反對稱的泊松括號——則為圖像識別和語言建模提供場的動力學。我們在四個領域評估 Metriplector，每個領域都使用從這個共享原語構建的特定任務架構，並逐步增強物理學：在迷宮尋路上 F1=1.0，從 15x15 的訓練網格推廣到未見的 39x39 網格；以零結構注入達到 97.2% 的精確數獨解決率；在 CIFAR-100 上獲得 81.03%，參數為 2.26M；在語言建模上以 1.182 位元/字節的效率，使用的訓練標記比 GPT 基線少 3.6 倍。
2603.29496.author: Dan Oprisa et.al.
